title: service-accounts — published events
description: Every SA lifecycle event fires after DB commit so downstream listeners never see uncommitted state. Payloads are readonly value objects (readonly class + readonly properties) — immutable by construction. Payloads carry `service_account_id` only; downstream services that need name/kid/tenant re-fetch through ServiceAccountRepository so redaction rules apply consistently.

events.name: ServiceAccountCreated
events.trigger: New ServiceAccount row inserted, first secret hashed, initial expires_at set. Fires exactly once per SA, ever.

payload.actor_platform_user_id: ULID (the platform_admin who provisioned the SA)

events.name: ServiceAccountEnabled
events.trigger: is_enabled transitioned from false to true. Fires only for genuine re-enables — the initial creation fires ServiceAccountCreated, not this.

payload.disabled_duration_days: integer (days between last disable and this enable)

events.name: ServiceAccountDisabled
events.trigger: is_enabled transitioned from true to false. Also fires as part of the ServiceAccountExpired + delete flows (each of those fires two events: the domain-specific one AND this).

events.name: ServiceAccountDeleted
events.trigger: Soft-delete (deleted_at set) OR hard-delete (PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob past-hold pass). Two dispatches per full-erasure lifecycle.

payload.reason: string | null (soft-delete captures the operator reason; hard-delete is null)

events.name: ServiceAccountSecretRotated
events.trigger: secret_hash mutated. Also updates secret_rotated_at + expires_at in the same transaction. Fires whether the trigger was manual (operator ran service-account:rotate) or scheduled (future RotateServiceAccountSecretJob).

events.name: ServiceAccountSecretRotationScheduled
events.trigger: NotifyRotationDueJob detected an SA whose expires_at falls inside the warning window (default 14d). Fires once per SA per week the SA is inside the window — debounced so the notification volume matches the weekly job cadence.

events.name: ServiceAccountJwtIssued
events.trigger: Token-exchange OR test-JWT issuance path. Payload captures the JWT's public metadata (jti, iat, exp, aud) NEVER the encoded token itself.
events.notes: This event fires on EVERY JWT issuance — potentially hundreds per second under peak machine traffic. The `queue` is `notifications` but the consumers are cheap (activity + metric); analytics is sampled to keep Segment ingestion manageable.

events.name: ServiceAccountUsageDetected
events.trigger: The FIRST request after a > `security.dormant_threshold_days` (default 30) silence. Distinct from the periodic Dormant Detected — this signals a wake-up which is compromise-adjacent. Debounced so subsequent requests within the same 24h window do NOT re-fire.

payload.user_agent_hash: string (SHA-256 of full UA truncated to 8 chars for label cardinality)

events.name: ServiceAccountExpired
events.trigger: PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob detected an SA past expires_at + grace_days_after_expiry with no rotation. The job soft-disables the SA in the same transaction; this event fires after commit.
